scenario: Echo SaaS runs a Camunda 8.8 Spring Boot service with 12 job workers that activate 7500 jobs per hour. The team must point the client at a non-production Camunda Cloud instance for a 2 week load test and is checking which camunda.client.cloud property the 8.8 reference defines for that purpose.
question: What does the 8.8 reference document camunda.client.cloud.domain as the property to change?

options.text: The 8.8 reference documents camunda.client.cloud.domain as the domain the Camunda client connects to, changed to target a non-production instance of Camunda Cloud.
The 8.8 reference documents camunda.client.cloud.domain as the cluster the Camunda client connects to, changed to target a non-production instance of Camunda Cloud.
The 8.8 reference documents camunda.client.cloud.domain as the region the Camunda client connects to, changed to target a non-production instance of Camunda Cloud.
The 8.8 reference documents camunda.client.cloud.domain as the tenant the Camunda client connects to, changed to target a non-production instance of Camunda Cloud.

a.text: Correct. The 8.8 reference says camunda.client.cloud.domain is the domain the Camunda client connects to, and is changed to target a non-production instance of Camunda Cloud.

b.text: Incorrect. Cluster targeting is handled by camunda.client.cloud.cluster-id; domain selects the DNS suffix the client connects to under SaaS mode.

c.text: Incorrect. Region targeting is handled by camunda.client.cloud.region; the domain property is a separate entry in the same section.

d.text: Incorrect. Tenant targeting is handled by tenant-ids; the camunda.client.cloud.domain property carries the DNS domain only.

explanation: The camunda.client.cloud.domain property in the 8.8 reference is the domain the Camunda client connects to. Changing it targets a non-production instance of Camunda Cloud while keeping other SaaS settings intact.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
